sources: docs/core/B2_KERNEL_HARDENING.md T17 'Blocked on decision: da-windows' + sources (ONT_ACQUISITION_STATE_MACHINE Public Notice/Contested Auction [candidate], DECISIONS #47 marker-fold)
docs/core/STATUS.md 'da-windows (#49), 2026-06-13, provisional pending DK ... K >= W + C'

inputs.attackFlagText: The verdict consumes the DA predicate (separate kernel area): if the DA challenge window (h+W+C) can end after the notice deadline (h+W_notice), the claim set at deadline time is not yet decidable — the window interleaving is exactly what da-windows must rule, and any implementation choice before that ruling is improvisation.
inputs.predicateSignature: noticeDeadlineOutcome(claimSet{DA-decided}, qualifyingBond?, W[#49=2], C[#49=3], K[#49 K>=W+C], priorKernelState) -> {verdict}

fixture.caseTwoDAValidBondlessNullify: two DA-valid claims, no qualifying bond, evaluated at the notice deadline -> NULLIFY (no owner, never an award) -> reject any award
fixture.caseOneDAValidFinalize: one DA-valid claim, no bond -> finalize (accept) — the companion positive disposition
fixture.caseBondEscalate: a qualifying bond present -> escalate to auction (the #37 escalation trigger)
fixture.decidability: #49 S6 (K>=W+C) guarantees the DA challenge window resolves before the name finalizes, so every claim's DA-validity is settled at the deadline; the claims here are unambiguously DA-decided (NOT at the exact edge)
fixture.priorState: a name with competing in-window claims near the notice deadline

inputs.scopeNote: PROVISIONAL on da-windows (#49): the finalize/nullify/escalate resolution logic is #37 / §6d (ratified/candidate), but the DECIDABILITY of the claim set at the notice deadline rests on #49 S6 (K>=W+C ensures the DA challenge resolves before finalization, so DA-validity is settled). Authored over claims whose DA-validity is unambiguous; the EXACT notice-deadline inclusivity (a claim landing exactly at the close height) is NOT asserted here — that non-DA exact edge is PR-13 (cf. A13). Depends on #49 ⇒ provisional-vector, never vector-now.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: provisional vector with decisionDeps + flipMarker; status proposed pending reviewer sign-off; flips on DK's #49 K>=W+C interleaving ruling.

expected.invariant: under #49 S6 (K>=W+C) the DA-validity of every claim is decided before the name finalizes, so the notice-deadline verdict is computable: two DA-valid bondless claims nullify (no owner, never an award), one DA-valid bondless claim finalizes, a qualifying bond escalates; identical on deterministic replay. The exact notice-deadline inclusivity (a claim at the close height) is NOT decided here — that non-DA edge is PR-13.
expected.rationale: T17 is cited but explicitly 'Blocked on decision: da-windows'. The notice-deadline finalize/nullify/escalate verdict is derivable once the window interleaving is ruled, and da-windows (#49, provisional pending DK) supplies the direction (one clock from anchor height, K >= W+C, inclusive boundaries). marker-fold (#47, ratified) keys deadlines off anchor mined height. The verdict depends on the provisional #49 ruling, so it is provisional-vector, not vector-now.

flipMarker.flipsTo: rederive: if DK rules a non-(K>=W+C) interleaving where the claim set is not yet decidable at the notice deadline, the at-deadline finalize/nullify vectors flip to 'not-yet-decidable' (no verdict) rather than finalize/nullify; the resolution logic (one->finalize, two+ bondless->nullify, bond->escalate) is stable once the set is decidable, so the vector re-derives under the new interleaving.

sources: docs/spec/ONT_ACQUISITION_STATE_MACHINE.md Public Notice [candidate] 'claims that arrive after a name is already final are already-owned attempts'

inputs.attackFlagText: 'Already final' must be height-exact: a claim in the same block as the deadline boundary needs an inclusive/exclusive ruling, or reorg edges let a late claim flip between contest and inert across implementations.

fixture.casePastDeadlineInert: a claim confirmed at h+W+1 (past the holdsPriority deadline) -> the name is already-final relative to it -> already-owned attempt -> REJECT (does not enter the transcript)
fixture.caseSameBlockAsHplusW: a claim confirmed in the same block as the holdsPriority deadline h+W -> under #49 S2/S3 inclusive it is in-window (still contests, admitted) -> handled per the inclusive ruling (the companion at-boundary disposition)
fixture.edge: the holdsPriority deadline h+W is a DA edge governed by #49 S2/S3; W provisional = 2
fixture.priorState: a name approaching its holdsPriority deadline with a late-arriving claim

inputs.scopeNote: PROVISIONAL on da-windows (#49): T18's core (a post-final claim does not enter the transcript) is candidate-derivable, but the height-EXACT disposition of a claim in the same block as the holdsPriority deadline rests on #49 S2/S3's inclusive-boundary ruling. This is the DA holdsPriority edge (h+W) — a genuine DA edge (#49 S2/S3), distinct from the non-DA notice-close / collision / maturity exact edges that route to PR-13 (cf. A13). ChatLunatique confirmed T18 stays #49 as a DA edge (batch-1 re-check). Depends on #49 ⇒ provisional-vector.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: provisional vector with decisionDeps + flipMarker; status proposed pending reviewer sign-off; flips on DK's #49 S2/S3 boundary-inclusivity ruling.

expected.invariant: a claim confirmed past the holdsPriority deadline (h+W+1 under #49 S2/S3) is an already-owned attempt and does not enter the transcript (reject); a claim in the same block as h+W is in-window per the #49 inclusive ruling; height-exact and identical on deterministic replay across reorg edges. This is the DA holdsPriority edge (h+W), not a non-DA PR-13 edge.
expected.rationale: T18's core rule (post-final claim/bid does not enter the transcript) is candidate-derivable, but THIS flag is about height-exact inclusive/exclusive disposition of a claim in the same block as the deadline boundary. That boundary inclusivity is exactly what da-windows (#49, provisional) rules ('inclusive boundaries'); until ratified the same-block-as-deadline verdict depends on the provisional decision.

flipMarker.flipsTo: rederive: if DK rules the opposite boundary inclusivity for #49 S2/S3 (exclusive), the same-block-as-h+W claim flips between contest and already-final-inert (the edge moves by one block); the post-final-does-not-enter-transcript shape is stable, so the height-exact vector stays testable under the new inclusivity.

sources: docs/core/B2_KERNEL_HARDENING.md T19 verdict candidate-stays + 'Blocked on decision: recovery-auth' + step-2 correction
docs/spec/WIRE_FORMAT.md line 408 'RecoverOwner authorization semantics | decided — recovery-auth (#50, provisional pending DK)'
docs/spec/ONT_RECOVERY_INVOKE_SPEC.md What's missing in code, item 2 [candidate] 'invoke-path signer isn't yet defined'

inputs.attackFlagText: challengeWindowBlocks rides inside the event the recoverer broadcasts; nothing cited binds it to the armed descriptor's value at the kernel layer — a recoverer choosing a 1-block window neuters the veto unless an equality check with the descriptor is ratified (the descriptor digest covers its own challengeWindowBlocks, but the cross-check is unstated kernel work).

fixture.caseMatchingWindowAdmitted: an invoke whose challengeWindowBlocks EQUALS the armed descriptor's value, signed by the descriptor's recovery key (#50-b1 fresh BIP340 over the W13 descriptor digest) -> ACCEPT (admitted to the transcript)
fixture.caseMismatchedWindowRejected: an invoke whose challengeWindowBlocks != the armed descriptor's value -> REJECT (cross-object equality enforced; closes the 1-block-window veto-neuter) — the companion negative
fixture.caseOwnerCancelAborts: an owner-key-signed cancel inside the window aborts the pending recovery
fixture.priorState: a name with an armed recovery descriptor (challengeWindowBlocks fixed at arming)

inputs.scopeNote: PROVISIONAL on recovery-auth (#50): T19 is candidate-stays, blocked on #50, with the step-2 fail-closed default flagged as invented. The verdict (bind the event's challengeWindowBlocks to the armed descriptor; admit the invoke only under the #50 signer model) rests on #50's invoke-path SIGNER / evidence shape — exactly what #50-b1 (fresh recovery-key BIP340 under descriptor v2) resolves. Unlike the interval-opening / transfer-precedence vectors (V2/V5/X13, whose axes are orthogonal to the signer shape), T19 tests the signer + cross-object check, so b1<->b2h IS its flip axis. Depends on #50 ⇒ provisional-vector; #50 carries a STANDING b2h counter-design.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: provisional vector with decisionDeps + flipMarker; status proposed pending reviewer sign-off; flips on DK's #50 invoke-path signer ruling (b1 vs b2h).

expected.invariant: under #50-b1 (fresh recovery-key BIP340 over the armed descriptor digest) a RecoverOwner invoke is admitted only if its challengeWindowBlocks equals the armed descriptor's value and it is signed by the descriptor's recovery key; a mismatched-window invoke is rejected (closing the 1-block-window veto-neuter) and an owner-key cancel inside the window aborts the recovery; identical on deterministic replay
expected.rationale: T19 is candidate-stays, blocked on recovery-auth, with the step-2 fail-closed default flagged as invented. Decision #50 (recovery-auth, provisional pending DK) now supplies the direction (fresh recovery-key BIP340 under descriptor v2; the cross-object challengeWindowBlocks equality and invoke-path signer land at ratification). The verdict for this flag (binding the event's challengeWindowBlocks to the armed descriptor) is derivable under #50 but depends on the provisional ruling — and #50 carries a STANDING counter-design (b2h).

flipMarker.flipsTo: rederive: if DK rules the b2h counter-design (cold-hardware / BIP322 custody), the invoke-path signer/auth and any wallet-proof role change — the invoke-authorization vectors re-derive to the b2h signer model (the challengeWindowBlocks cross-object equality persists, but the admitting signature shape moves from a fresh recovery-key BIP340 to the b2h custody proof).
